tools.title: Booking service health probe
tools.description: Liveness probe for the greenfield booking service. Returns service identity, ok flag, session source, explicit demo-auth posture, demoCustomerId for local/demo harness proof, venueProductionReady=false, and timestamp. No authentication required — anonymous callers receive authenticated: false. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service router.ts.

tools.title: Create a bookable context
tools.description: Creates a bookable context — the managed bookable thing (an event, a venue_schedule like a tee sheet or restaurant service, or a resource like a simulator bay). Provide its kind, display name, and timezone (required); config defaults to empty and slug is optional. The owning organization is taken from your session — never send it in the body. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

tools.title: Project a legacy event context
tools.description: Projects a real Sonik event into booking as bookable_context.kind=event using externalEventRef as a soft shadow key. This operation is idempotent per organization + externalEventRef, refreshes mutable display/config fields on replay, and does not make booking authoritative for legacy event ticketing, approvals, analytics, or commerce. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

tools.title: Get a bookable context
tools.description: Returns one bookable context by id. Only contexts in your organization are visible — a context owned by another org is indistinguishable from a missing one (both 404). Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

tools.title: Update a bookable context
tools.description: Updates a context's mutable fields — name, config, and/or slug. The context's kind cannot be changed: a kind determines how its schedule rules are interpreted, so changing it would orphan the rule semantics. Send only the fields you want to change. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

tools.title: Create a schedule rule
tools.description: Adds a schedule rule to a context. A rule is an open config envelope expressing an effective-date range, a priority, and pacing/window/turn-time settings (e.g. opens/closes, slotMinutes, capacity); higher-priority rules win where effective windows overlap. Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

tools.title: Get computed availability for a context
tools.description: Returns the computed availability slots for a context over a date range (from/to). This is THE law of the booking framework: availability is computed from the context's schedule rules every time it is requested — it is never stored as slots. Each returned slot carries its start, end, and remaining capacity after existing bookings. Optional partySize/source/resource filters let the read hide slots that active booking-window or party policies would reject; the response remains a bare slot array without pricing or reason-code expansion. This read is NOT tracked as an attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

tools.title: Create a booking
tools.description: Creates a booking against a bookable context for a customer over a time window. The same shape backs a tee time, a dinner reservation, or any other booking — the context decides what it means. Supply a clientRequestId to make the create idempotent: retrying with the same key returns the existing booking with 200 rather than creating a duplicate (the Worker-retry defense). The organization is taken from your session. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

tools.title: Cancel a booking
tools.description: Cancels a booking, moving it to 'cancelled'. Cancelling an already-cancelled booking is a conflict (409) — the transition is illegal. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

accessibility.label: Cancel a booking
accessibility.description: Cancels a booking, moving it to 'cancelled'. Cancelling an already-cancelled booking is a conflict (409) — the transition is illegal. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

tools.title: Confirm a booking
tools.description: Confirms a booking, moving it from 'booked' to 'confirmed'. Confirming a booking that is not in 'booked' status is a conflict (409).

accessibility.label: Confirm a booking
accessibility.description: Confirms a booking, moving it from 'booked' to 'confirmed'. Confirming a booking that is not in 'booked' status is a conflict (409).

tools.title: Reschedule a booking
tools.description: Moves a booking to a new time window on the same booking — a first-class operation, not a cancel-and-rebook. The booking keeps its id, party, and resource assignments. The new ends must be strictly after the new starts. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

tools.title: List party members for a booking
tools.description: Returns the booking's current active party roster. Removed members remain tombstoned for audit/retry safety but are excluded from this current-roster response.

tools.title: Add a party member to a booking
tools.description: Adds a generic party member to a booking. Provide a customerId for a known guest, or omit it for a walk-in/guest — a guest without a customer record is identified by displayName alone and is never written as CRM/customer profile data. Supplying clientRequestId makes retried adds return the existing party row instead of duplicating the roster. Returns the booking's current active party roster.

tools.title: Remove a party member from a booking
tools.description: Removes a generic party member from a booking by party-member id by tombstoning the booking_party row. Repeating the same remove for an already-removed party row is idempotent and returns the current active roster without emitting a duplicate lifecycle event. This mutates booking operational roster truth only; it does not delete or update CRM/customer profile data.

tools.title: Update a customer's contact fields
tools.description: Updates the contact fields (name, phone, email) on one of your customers. Send only the fields you want to change. phone, if supplied, must stay non-empty — the spine column is NOT NULL and cannot be cleared; email may be set to null to clear it. This edits the CUSTOMER row only — it never mutates a user (organizers edit customers, never users). 404 if the customer is not in your organization. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

tools.title: Open (or fetch) a member's house account
tools.description: Operator op. Opens a house account — a member's 'tab' — for a customer, or returns the existing one. Idempotent by the one-account-per-(org, customer) rule: calling it twice for the same member returns the same account with 200, never a conflict. Responds with the account and its computed balance. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

tools.description: Operator op. Returns a paginated page of a member's ledger entries, newest first, filterable by kind (charge / payment / adjustment), occurred-at window, and linked booking. A tee time and a dinner both appear here as charges against the one tab. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

tools.title: Post a charge, payment, or adjustment
tools.description: Operator op. Records one ledger entry on a member's tab: a charge (positive — they owe more, e.g. a tee time or dinner), a payment (negative — they paid the tab down), or an adjustment (either sign — a comp or correction). amount_cents must be nonzero. Optionally links the entry to the booking that produced it. Ledger-only: this records money, it does not capture or settle any payment (no payment rails are integrated). Intended as the future Layer-1 monetary-signal emission point (capture-only). Responds with the created entry. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

Optionally links the entry to the booking that produced it. Ledger-only: this records money, it does not capture or settle any payment (no payment rails are integrated). Intended as the future Layer-1 monetary-signal emission point (capture-only). Responds with the created entry. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

tools.title: Read a member's statement / F&B-minimum progress
tools.description: Operator op. Returns a computed statement for a member's tab over an optional occurred-at period: the net balance, per-kind subtotals (charges, payments, adjustments), and an entry count. The charge subtotal over a billing period is the food-and-beverage-minimum progress number — how much the member has spent toward their minimum. Every figure is a read-time aggregate over the ledger; nothing here is stored. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

commands.title: Create a schedule rule
commands.description: Adds a schedule rule to a context. A rule is an open config envelope expressing an effective-date range, a priority, and pacing/window/turn-time settings (e.g. opens/closes, slotMinutes, capacity); higher-priority rules win where effective windows overlap. Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Get computed availability for a context
commands.description: Returns the computed availability slots for a context over a date range (from/to). This is THE law of the booking framework: availability is computed from the context's schedule rules every time it is requested — it is never stored as slots. Each returned slot carries its start, end, and remaining capacity after existing bookings. Optional partySize/source/resource filters let the read hide slots that active booking-window or party policies would reject; the response remains a bare slot array without pricing or reason-code expansion. This read is NOT tracked as an attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Create a schedule rule
commands.description: Adds a schedule rule to a context. A rule is an open config envelope expressing an effective-date range, a priority, and pacing/window/turn-time settings (e.g. opens/closes, slotMinutes, capacity); higher-priority rules win where effective windows overlap. Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Get computed availability for a context
commands.description: Returns the computed availability slots for a context over a date range (from/to). This is THE law of the booking framework: availability is computed from the context's schedule rules every time it is requested — it is never stored as slots. Each returned slot carries its start, end, and remaining capacity after existing bookings. Optional partySize/source/resource filters let the read hide slots that active booking-window or party policies would reject; the response remains a bare slot array without pricing or reason-code expansion. This read is NOT tracked as an attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

The organization is taken from your session. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

commands.title: Post a charge, payment, or adjustment
commands.description: Operator op. Records one ledger entry on a member's tab: a charge (positive — they owe more, e.g. a tee time or dinner), a payment (negative — they paid the tab down), or an adjustment (either sign — a comp or correction). amount_cents must be nonzero. Optionally links the entry to the booking that produced it. Ledger-only: this records money, it does not capture or settle any payment (no payment rails are integrated). Intended as the future Layer-1 monetary-signal emission point (capture-only). Responds with the created entry. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

commands.title: Read a member's statement / F&B-minimum progress

The charge subtotal over a billing period is the food-and-beverage-minimum progress number — how much the member has spent toward their minimum. Every figure is a read-time aggregate over the ledger; nothing here is stored. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

commands.title: File a loyalty claim

commands.title: Resolve a loyalty claim by ledger replay
commands.description: Runs ledger replay against a claim's source-event candidate. If the replay checks out, the earn posts itself as a new ledger entry carrying a reference back to the claim and the claim closes resolved_posted; otherwise it closes resolved_no_earn with no entry posted. Never accepts a manual point amount. Resolving an already-resolved claim is a status-transition conflict. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

commands.title: Create a bookable context
commands.description: Creates a bookable context — the managed bookable thing (an event, a venue_schedule like a tee sheet or restaurant service, or a resource like a simulator bay). Provide its kind, display name, and timezone (required); config defaults to empty and slug is optional. The owning organization is taken from your session — never send it in the body. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: List bookable contexts

accessible.label: List bookable contexts

commands.description: Projects a real Sonik event into booking as bookable_context.kind=event using externalEventRef as a soft shadow key. This operation is idempotent per organization + externalEventRef, refreshes mutable display/config fields on replay, and does not make booking authoritative for legacy event ticketing, approvals, analytics, or commerce. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Get a bookable context
commands.description: Returns one bookable context by id. Only contexts in your organization are visible — a context owned by another org is indistinguishable from a missing one (both 404). Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

accessible.label: Get a bookable context
accessible.description: Returns one bookable context by id. Only contexts in your organization are visible — a context owned by another org is indistinguishable from a missing one (both 404). Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Update a bookable context
commands.description: Updates a context's mutable fields — name, config, and/or slug. The context's kind cannot be changed: a kind determines how its schedule rules are interpreted, so changing it would orphan the rule semantics. Send only the fields you want to change. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

accessible.label: Update a bookable context
accessible.description: Updates a context's mutable fields — name, config, and/or slug. The context's kind cannot be changed: a kind determines how its schedule rules are interpreted, so changing it would orphan the rule semantics. Send only the fields you want to change. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Create a schedule rule
commands.description: Adds a schedule rule to a context. A rule is an open config envelope expressing an effective-date range, a priority, and pacing/window/turn-time settings (e.g. opens/closes, slotMinutes, capacity); higher-priority rules win where effective windows overlap. Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

accessible.label: Create a schedule rule
accessible.description: Adds a schedule rule to a context. A rule is an open config envelope expressing an effective-date range, a priority, and pacing/window/turn-time settings (e.g. opens/closes, slotMinutes, capacity); higher-priority rules win where effective windows overlap. Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: List a context's schedule rules
commands.description: Lists every schedule rule attached to a context, in the order they were created. Only your organization's contexts are visible. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

accessible.label: List a context's schedule rules
accessible.description: Lists every schedule rule attached to a context, in the order they were created. Only your organization's contexts are visible. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Update a schedule rule
commands.description: Replaces a schedule rule's config envelope. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

accessible.label: Update a schedule rule
accessible.description: Replaces a schedule rule's config envelope. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Delete a schedule rule
commands.description: Removes a schedule rule from a context. Future availability computations stop applying it immediately. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

accessible.label: Delete a schedule rule
accessible.description: Removes a schedule rule from a context. Future availability computations stop applying it immediately. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

commands.title: Get computed availability for a context
commands.description: Returns the computed availability slots for a context over a date range (from/to). This is THE law of the booking framework: availability is computed from the context's schedule rules every time it is requested — it is never stored as slots. Each returned slot carries its start, end, and remaining capacity after existing bookings. Optional partySize/source/resource filters let the read hide slots that active booking-window or party policies would reject; the response remains a bare slot array without pricing or reason-code expansion. This read is NOT tracked as an attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

accessible.label: Get computed availability for a context
accessible.description: Returns the computed availability slots for a context over a date range (from/to). This is THE law of the booking framework: availability is computed from the context's schedule rules every time it is requested — it is never stored as slots. Each returned slot carries its start, end, and remaining capacity after existing bookings. Optional partySize/source/resource filters let the read hide slots that active booking-window or party policies would reject; the response remains a bare slot array without pricing or reason-code expansion. This read is NOT tracked as an attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.
accessible.actionLabel: Get computed availability for a context

commands.title: Create a booking
commands.description: Creates a booking against a bookable context for a customer over a time window. The same shape backs a tee time, a dinner reservation, or any other booking — the context decides what it means. Supply a clientRequestId to make the create idempotent: retrying with the same key returns the existing booking with 200 rather than creating a duplicate (the Worker-retry defense). The organization is taken from your session. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

accessible.label: Create a booking
accessible.description: Creates a booking against a bookable context for a customer over a time window. The same shape backs a tee time, a dinner reservation, or any other booking — the context decides what it means. Supply a clientRequestId to make the create idempotent: retrying with the same key returns the existing booking with 200 rather than creating a duplicate (the Worker-retry defense). The organization is taken from your session. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

commands.title: List bookings
commands.description: Lists bookings in your organization, optionally filtered by context, customer, status, and a start-time date range. Org scoping is implicit — you only ever see your own organization's bookings.

accessible.label: List bookings
accessible.description: Lists bookings in your organization, optionally filtered by context, customer, status, and a start-time date range. Org scoping is implicit — you only ever see your own organization's bookings.

commands.title: Get a booking

accessible.label: Get a booking

commands.title: Cancel a booking
commands.description: Cancels a booking, moving it to 'cancelled'. Cancelling an already-cancelled booking is a conflict (409) — the transition is illegal. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

accessible.label: Cancel a booking
accessible.description: Cancels a booking, moving it to 'cancelled'. Cancelling an already-cancelled booking is a conflict (409) — the transition is illegal. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

commands.title: Confirm a booking
commands.description: Confirms a booking, moving it from 'booked' to 'confirmed'. Confirming a booking that is not in 'booked' status is a conflict (409).

accessible.label: Confirm a booking
accessible.description: Confirms a booking, moving it from 'booked' to 'confirmed'. Confirming a booking that is not in 'booked' status is a conflict (409).

commands.title: Reschedule a booking
commands.description: Moves a booking to a new time window on the same booking — a first-class operation, not a cancel-and-rebook. The booking keeps its id, party, and resource assignments. The new ends must be strictly after the new starts. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

accessible.label: Reschedule a booking

commands.title: List party members for a booking

accessible.description: Returns the booking's current active party roster. Removed members remain tombstoned for audit/retry safety but are excluded from this current-roster response.

commands.title: Add a party member to a booking
commands.description: Adds a generic party member to a booking. Provide a customerId for a known guest, or omit it for a walk-in/guest — a guest without a customer record is identified by displayName alone and is never written as CRM/customer profile data. Supplying clientRequestId makes retried adds return the existing party row instead of duplicating the roster. Returns the booking's current active party roster.

accessible.label: Add a party member to a booking

commands.title: Remove a party member from a booking

Repeating the same remove for an already-removed party row is idempotent and returns the current active roster without emitting a duplicate lifecycle event. This mutates booking operational roster truth only; it does not delete or update CRM/customer profile data.

commands.title: Update a customer's contact fields
commands.description: Updates the contact fields (name, phone, email) on one of your customers. Send only the fields you want to change. phone, if supplied, must stay non-empty — the spine column is NOT NULL and cannot be cleared; email may be set to null to clear it. This edits the CUSTOMER row only — it never mutates a user (organizers edit customers, never users). 404 if the customer is not in your organization. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

accessible.label: Update a customer's contact fields
accessible.description: Updates the contact fields (name, phone, email) on one of your customers. Send only the fields you want to change. phone, if supplied, must stay non-empty — the spine column is NOT NULL and cannot be cleared; email may be set to null to clear it. This edits the CUSTOMER row only — it never mutates a user (organizers edit customers, never users). 404 if the customer is not in your organization. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

commands.title: Open (or fetch) a member's house account
commands.description: Operator op. Opens a house account — a member's 'tab' — for a customer, or returns the existing one. Idempotent by the one-account-per-(org, customer) rule: calling it twice for the same member returns the same account with 200, never a conflict. Responds with the account and its computed balance. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

accessible.label: Open (or fetch) a member's house account
accessible.description: Operator op. Opens a house account — a member's 'tab' — for a customer, or returns the existing one. Idempotent by the one-account-per-(org, customer) rule: calling it twice for the same member returns the same account with 200, never a conflict. Responds with the account and its computed balance. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.
accessible.actionLabel: Open (or fetch) a member's house account

commands.title: Get a house account with its balance
commands.description: Operator op. Returns a member's house account plus its running balance. The balance is computed live as the sum of all ledger entries — it is never stored — so it is always consistent with the tab. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

accessible.label: Get a house account with its balance
accessible.description: Operator op. Returns a member's house account plus its running balance. The balance is computed live as the sum of all ledger entries — it is never stored — so it is always consistent with the tab. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.
accessible.actionLabel: Get a house account with its balance

commands.title: List a house account's ledger entries
commands.description: Operator op. Returns a paginated page of a member's ledger entries, newest first, filterable by kind (charge / payment / adjustment), occurred-at window, and linked booking. A tee time and a dinner both appear here as charges against the one tab. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

accessible.label: List a house account's ledger entries
accessible.description: Operator op. Returns a paginated page of a member's ledger entries, newest first, filterable by kind (charge / payment / adjustment), occurred-at window, and linked booking. A tee time and a dinner both appear here as charges against the one tab. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.
accessible.actionLabel: List a house account's ledger entries

commands.title: Post a charge, payment, or adjustment
commands.description: Operator op. Records one ledger entry on a member's tab: a charge (positive — they owe more, e.g. a tee time or dinner), a payment (negative — they paid the tab down), or an adjustment (either sign — a comp or correction). amount_cents must be nonzero. Optionally links the entry to the booking that produced it. Ledger-only: this records money, it does not capture or settle any payment (no payment rails are integrated). Intended as the future Layer-1 monetary-signal emission point (capture-only). Responds with the created entry. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

accessible.label: Post a charge, payment, or adjustment
accessible.description: Operator op. Records one ledger entry on a member's tab: a charge (positive — they owe more, e.g. a tee time or dinner), a payment (negative — they paid the tab down), or an adjustment (either sign — a comp or correction). amount_cents must be nonzero. Optionally links the entry to the booking that produced it. Ledger-only: this records money, it does not capture or settle any payment (no payment rails are integrated). Intended as the future Layer-1 monetary-signal emission point (capture-only). Responds with the created entry. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.
accessible.actionLabel: Post a charge, payment, or adjustment

commands.title: Read a member's statement / F&B-minimum progress
commands.description: Operator op. Returns a computed statement for a member's tab over an optional occurred-at period: the net balance, per-kind subtotals (charges, payments, adjustments), and an entry count. The charge subtotal over a billing period is the food-and-beverage-minimum progress number — how much the member has spent toward their minimum. Every figure is a read-time aggregate over the ledger; nothing here is stored. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

accessible.label: Read a member's statement / F&B-minimum progress
accessible.description: Operator op. Returns a computed statement for a member's tab over an optional occurred-at period: the net balance, per-kind subtotals (charges, payments, adjustments), and an entry count. The charge subtotal over a billing period is the food-and-beverage-minimum progress number — how much the member has spent toward their minimum. Every figure is a read-time aggregate over the ledger; nothing here is stored. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.
accessible.actionLabel: Read a member's statement / F&B-minimum progress
